jokes.text: D'oh! (SCREAMING) (BRAKES SCREECHING)
jokes.setup: Opening credits couch gag sequence
jokes.punchline: Homer's signature frustrated exclamation followed by crash sounds
jokes.explanation: Homer's iconic catchphrase - high character integration and quotability but routine by this point in series

jokes.text: Bart, that creepy kid is here.
jokes.setup: Homer announcing Milhouse's arrival
jokes.punchline: Calling Milhouse 'that creepy kid' instead of using his name
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer insensitivity - economical and character-appropriate insult

jokes.text: That's me. (READING) Oh!
jokes.setup: Milhouse reading about a rub-on tattoo 'for that special someone'
jokes.punchline: Milhouse identifying himself as that 'special someone'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Milhouse moment - pathetic self-identification with romantic delusion

jokes.text: it sounds like a motorcycle gang. And we don't have backup! We'd better lay low. But Chief, what if they like pizza? Way ahead of you, Lou.
jokes.setup: Police hear bike sounds and think it's a motorcycle gang
jokes.punchline: Chief's illogical solution involves pizza as gang deterrent
jokes.explanation: Classic Wiggum non-logic - character-perfect absurd police reasoning

jokes.text: That is one bitchin' bike. Daddy said a cuss word! Lighten up, Roddy.
jokes.setup: Ned admiring Bart's bike
jokes.punchline: Ned using mild profanity, Rod calling it out, Ned being dismissive
jokes.explanation: Excellent character subversion - religious Ned swearing and dismissing his son's concern

jokes.text: Thanks for the tassels. My mom can wear these on her boobs at work.
jokes.setup: Nelson taking Bart's bike tassels
jokes.punchline: Inappropriately sexual comment about his mother's job
jokes.explanation: Dark Nelson humor - implies his mother's profession while maintaining kid-show boundaries

jokes.text: A ten-speed bike? What did your mother say? She said, 'Yes.' MARGE: I said no! I'm confused. Which is it? MARGE: it's no! His old bike is fine. Yeah, the kitchen lady's right.
jokes.setup: Bart trying to manipulate parents about getting new bike
jokes.punchline: Homer calling Marge 'the kitchen lady' while deferring to her
jokes.explanation: Good family dynamic joke - Homer's casual sexism while actually being reasonable

jokes.text: I see. So if my old bike didn't work, I would automatically get a new one? That's right. No questions asked. (LAUGHS)
jokes.setup: Bart leading Homer into logical trap about bike replacement
jokes.punchline: Homer agreeing without realizing implication, Bart's knowing laugh
jokes.explanation: Well-crafted setup for bike destruction - shows Bart's cunning and Homer's gullibility

jokes.text: Boy, I'm really gonna miss you. We've really had some great... Ooh! A Mercedes!
jokes.setup: Bart giving sentimental goodbye to his bike
jokes.punchline: Immediately distracted by fancy car before finishing thought
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection - Bart's fake sentimentality immediately undercut by materialistic distraction

jokes.text: What the Halle Berry?
jokes.setup: Dr. Hibbert witnessing bike accident
jokes.punchline: Substituting actress name for profanity in exclamation
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay on 'What the hell' using contemporary celebrity name

jokes.text: This was a wakeup call. From now on, I'll keep my eyes on the road and off my Kool & the Gang air freshener. Celebration's over, boys.
jokes.setup: Dr. Hibbert promising to drive more safely after accident
jokes.punchline: Specific reference to Kool & the Gang air freshener and 'Celebration' pun
jokes.explanation: Excellent specific detail and musical pun - absurd air freshener choice with perfect song reference

jokes.text: It's called 'Cat Math.' 'Four paws, plus one tail,' 'plus nine lives equals one special cat.' 'One special cat minus nine lives equals one sad little girl.'
jokes.setup: Lisa writing poem for dead cat
jokes.punchline: Mathematical approach to emotional pet loss
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa character moment - intellectualizing grief through mathematics while remaining genuinely touching

jokes.text: Oh, Cinnamon! It should've been me who chewed through that extension cord!
jokes.setup: Marge comforting Lisa about pet loss, mentions her own guinea pig
jokes.punchline: Dramatic declaration of survivor's guilt over guinea pig's electrocution
jokes.explanation: Dark humor escalation - Marge's melodramatic guilt over guinea pig death is both absurd and touching

jokes.text: Here we go. Now it starts with the fees.
jokes.setup: Bike shop employee mentions assembly fee
jokes.punchline: Homer's weary recognition of retail upselling tactics
jokes.explanation: Relatable consumer frustration - Homer's resigned recognition of retail tactics

jokes.text: I can make a bike. I made you. Yeah, great workmanship. Hey, that's gonna win you a lot of bar bets someday.
jokes.setup: Homer claiming he can build bike because he made Bart
jokes.punchline: Bart's sarcastic comeback about Homer's 'workmanship,' Homer's oblivious positive response
jokes.explanation: Good father-son dynamic - Bart's cutting insult met with Homer's oblivious encouragement

jokes.text: Man, this is so confusing. What the hell is this?
jokes.setup: Homer attempting to assemble bike
jokes.punchline: Immediate frustration and confusion with simple task
jokes.explanation: Standard Homer incompetence moment. In character but predictable.

jokes.text: I'm riding a unicycle with my pants down. This should be every boy's dream. Ha-ha! Your dad's not handy.
jokes.setup: Homer's bike assembly creates unicycle with Bart's pants falling
jokes.punchline: Bart's ironic observation about dream scenario, Nelson's mockery
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy with Bart's wry self-awareness of humiliating situation

jokes.text: Son, are you okay? I brought a homemade first aid kit! It's spring-loaded for quick access. (SCREAMING)
jokes.setup: Homer trying to help injured Bart with first aid kit
jokes.punchline: Homer's 'improved' first aid kit malfunctions and presumably hurts him
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy escalation - Homer's helpful intentions backfire with typical incompetence

jokes.text: 'And you won't be teed off when you come in' 'because our steaks are cooked to par-fection.' 'Our leader bar is filled with scrumptious...' Two more pages? I'm not reading this.
jokes.setup: TV announcer reading golf-themed restaurant advertisement copy
jokes.punchline: Announcer breaking character and refusing to read overly long, pun-heavy copy
jokes.explanation: Smart meta-humor about bad advertising copy and announcer's professional limits

jokes.text: But you're good at other things like eating while driving! That's something! And nobody gets madder at the news!
jokes.setup: Bart trying to console Homer about not being mechanical
jokes.punchline: Bart's 'compliments' are Homer's worst traits presented as positives
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Bart's well-intentioned but misguided attempt at encouragement

jokes.text: It's written by a Rabbi, who surfs!
jokes.setup: Marge defending her self-help book's wisdom
jokes.punchline: Rabbi who surfs as credential for pet grief counseling
jokes.explanation: Absurd but specific detail - the incongruous combination makes it memorable and funny

jokes.text: Too fluffy, too Siamese, too needy, too stuck up. Infected eye, clearly a skunk.
jokes.setup: Lisa rejecting cats at animal shelter
jokes.punchline: Escalating from personality complaints to medical issues to species confusion
jokes.explanation: Good escalation structure - moves from subjective to objective to absurd rejection criteria

jokes.text: There's nothing worse than the look on a boy's face when he says, 'Dad, I don't think you can build a fully functional robot.'
jokes.setup: Homer reflecting on parental disappointment
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific scenario showing his insecurity
jokes.explanation: Character-specific insecurity with absurdly specific focus on robot-building

jokes.text: If you can't build a robot, be a robot. That's it!
jokes.setup: Homer remembering his father's advice
jokes.punchline: Absurd father-son wisdom about becoming a robot
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist wisdom - sounds profound but makes no sense, perfectly Homer Sr.

jokes.text: KRUSTY CLOCK: It's Tuesday, the first. If you live in Krusty Brand low-income housing, your rent is due!
jokes.setup: Novelty clock announcing time
jokes.punchline: Clock also serves as rent collection notice for poor housing
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about poverty exploitation through novelty clock - clever world-building detail

jokes.text: 'Dear Bart, if this robot doesn't prove I love you,' 'then you can both go to hell.'
jokes.setup: Homer's note to Bart about the robot
jokes.punchline: Loving sentiment that turns hostile and includes the robot as target
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer letter - starts loving, becomes hostile, includes inanimate object as target. Excellent character writing.

jokes.text: Oh, the beer's gone. I'll have to suck it out of the shag.
jokes.setup: Robot smashes Homer's beer six-pack
jokes.punchline: Homer's determination to recover beer from carpet fibers
jokes.explanation: Pure Homer desperation - perfect character integration but relies on grossness for humor

jokes.text: You plop the cat food down, You toss the tin can out, You drop the worm pills in, And you stir it all about, You add a lot of lovin' and you serve it to your cat, That's what it's all...
jokes.setup: Lisa singing while preparing cat food
jokes.punchline: Parody of 'Hokey Pokey' adapted for cat care routine
jokes.explanation: Cute parody song - shows Lisa's nurturing nature but not particularly strong comedy

jokes.text: You're a Buddhist. So you know your cats are now reincarnated as a higher form of life. Like a dog or a snowman.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to comfort Lisa about dead cat with Buddhism
jokes.punchline: Homer's misunderstanding of reincarnation hierarchy including snowman
jokes.explanation: Excellent Homer religious ignorance - snowman as higher life form is perfectly absurd

jokes.text: Are you here with your children, sir? Yeah, my two kids, Screw and You.
jokes.setup: Security asking Comic Book Guy about accompanying children
jokes.punchline: Comic Book Guy's rude response disguised as children's names
jokes.explanation: Classic Comic Book Guy rudeness - economical insult with character-appropriate superiority

jokes.text: He's killing him softly with his saw. Killing him softly? With his saw.
jokes.setup: Robot fight commentary
jokes.punchline: Parody of 'Killing Me Softly' adapted to robot combat
jokes.explanation: Simple song parody - works but not particularly clever or memorable

jokes.text: You got to name me. You should be glad I did. Your father wanted to call you 'Bartzina.'
jokes.setup: Lisa wanting to name her cat
jokes.punchline: Homer's proposed feminine version of Bart's name
jokes.explanation: Good character detail - Homer's terrible naming sense with lazy feminine suffix

jokes.text: 'Coltrane, you were with us only briefly' 'but we will always have the ride home from the shelter.' And, um, I guess that's it. Amen.
jokes.setup: Lisa giving eulogy for second dead cat
jokes.punchline: Pathetically brief relationship summarized in awkward funeral speech
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy about pet mortality - Lisa's dignified attempt at eulogy for barely-known cat

jokes.text: And Lord, if you think I'm making lemon bars for your bake sale Sunday, you better stop killing our cats. Mom, I'm not sure God responds to threats and intimidation. It's the only way to talk to bullies.
jokes.setup: Marge adding to Lisa's prayer
jokes.punchline: Marge threatening God over cat deaths, calling God a bully
jokes.explanation: Excellent Marge moment - escalating from prayer to divine threat, calling God a bully is perfectly in character

jokes.text: It's the only way to talk to bullies.
jokes.setup: Lisa questioning threatening God
jokes.punchline: Marge categorizing God as a bully requiring tough approach
jokes.explanation: Brilliant theological take from Marge. Treating God like a schoolyard bully is perfectly irreverent and in-character.

jokes.text: In my day, mechanical men had funnel heads and showed respect! Then it all changed when they got the vote and started tinkering with our memories.
jokes.setup: Grampa complaining about modern robots
jokes.punchline: Nonsensical timeline where robots got voting rights and memory manipulation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Grampa ramble - starts with real nostalgia, becomes science fiction paranoia

jokes.text: There he sits with a pen and a yellow pad, What a handsome lad That's my boy, B-R-L-F-Q spells mom and dad, Well, that ain't too bad 'Cause that's my boy
jokes.setup: Homer singing sentimental song about Bart
jokes.punchline: Random letters that don't spell 'mom and dad' but Homer accepts them
jokes.explanation: Homer's illogical pride - accepting nonsense spelling because of paternal love

jokes.text: Well, I've been busy, Son. They really need me over at the nuclear 'plank.'
jokes.setup: Homer explaining his absence to Bart
jokes.punchline: Mispronouncing 'plant' as 'plank'
jokes.explanation: Simple malapropism - classic Homer mispronunciation but not particularly clever

jokes.text: Smashius Clay, AKA Killhammad Aieee!
jokes.setup: Announcing champion robot fighter
jokes.punchline: Boxing parody names combining Muhammad Ali with violent robot themes
jokes.explanation: Solid boxing parody - good puns on famous names adapted to robot combat

jokes.text: There are many people I'd like to thank for this award. My wife Anjelica Houston. This is for you, Angie. We did it! ROBOT: Speeches cannot be longer than 30 seconds. (SCREAMS)
jokes.setup: Bart's nightmare about winning award and giving acceptance speech
jokes.punchline: Dream robot enforces time limit violently
jokes.explanation: Dream sequence absurdity - robot enforcing speech limits fits nightmare logic

jokes.text: I just installed a chip that makes it 10% more bloodthirsty.
jokes.setup: Homer needing to tell Bart something important about robot
jokes.punchline: Homer lying about robot upgrade instead of revealing he's inside
jokes.explanation: Good character lie - Homer's specific percentage makes the fabrication sound technical

jokes.text: Hey, hey! These cats'd rather take their chances in the back room than go home with you.
jokes.setup: Lisa trying to adopt another cat
jokes.punchline: Even cats facing euthanasia prefer death to Lisa's care
jokes.explanation: Dark humor escalation - cats preferring death is harsh but effective callback to Lisa's pet mortality rate

jokes.text: That's what we in the business call a 'biggie.' Business? What business? The business of being a dad.
jokes.setup: Homer pulling metal from his arm
jokes.punchline: Homer treating fatherhood as professional expertise
jokes.explanation: Good Homer delusion - treating parenting as professional specialization

jokes.text: I'm much happier petting this leaf.
jokes.setup: Lisa giving up on cat ownership
jokes.punchline: Settles for petting inanimate object instead
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist solution to pet death problem. Lisa's resignation to leaf-petting is sweetly absurd.

jokes.text: Look, you don't want to get involved with a girl like me. My cats have a nasty habit of waking up dead. Now go. Cough me out of your life like a bad fur ball.
jokes.setup: Lisa talking to cat who seems to like her
jokes.punchline: Lisa's melodramatic self-rejection speech to cat using cat metaphors
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment - Lisa's dramatic self-awareness with perfect cat metaphor conclusion

jokes.text: That's really a cheat, isn't it? I guess you're right, Principal Tamzarian.
jokes.setup: Lisa acknowledging the cheat of renaming the cat
jokes.punchline: Skinner reference to his own identity retcon episode
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-callback - Lisa's acknowledgment of cheating connects to controversial Skinner identity episode, perfect self-aware humor

jokes.text: That moan sounded almost human. HOMER: The hell it did.
jokes.setup: Bart noticing Homer's too-human robot sounds
jokes.punchline: Homer's defensive denial while still in robot suit
jokes.explanation: Good dramatic irony - Homer's human response proves Bart's point while denying it

jokes.text: Even though you're the sole provider for a family of five!
jokes.setup: Bart praising Homer for risking his life fighting robots
jokes.punchline: Bart's realization makes Homer's risk-taking seem irresponsible rather than heroic
jokes.explanation: Smart observation - Bart's enthusiasm undercuts itself by highlighting Homer's irresponsibility

jokes.text: That robot has given birth to a man!
jokes.setup: Homer emerging from broken robot suit
jokes.punchline: Describing it as robot giving birth to human
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist description of visual. The birth metaphor works well for the reveal.

jokes.text: That robot has given birth to a man!
jokes.setup: Homer emerging from destroyed robot suit
jokes.punchline: Treating mechanical breakdown as biological process
jokes.explanation: Absurd biological metaphor for mechanical revelation - works but not exceptional

jokes.text: He knows just how I like my martinis. Full of alcohol.
jokes.setup: Frink's robot serving Homer a drink
jokes.punchline: Homer's simple preference for alcoholic content in martinis
jokes.explanation: Simple Homer preference - understated way to express his alcoholism

jokes.text: Bart, all that button ever did was send a mild electric shock up my backside. Why did you make it do that? To keep me focused.
jokes.setup: Homer explaining the robot's control system
jokes.punchline: Bart's sadistic motivation for shocking his father
jokes.explanation: Good character revelation - Bart's casual cruelty presented as helpful motivation

jokes.text: Show me where in the rulebook it says that a human can't be a robot. Right here. Rule One.
jokes.setup: Homer's confident challenge about human-robot rules
jokes.punchline: Official immediately finding the exact rule
jokes.explanation: Good deflation comedy - Homer's confidence immediately undercut by obvious rule

jokes.text: Well, you did disappear a lot but I've gone whole summers without seeing you. (LAUGHS) Yeah. I'm pretty unreliable.
jokes.setup: Bart asked if he suspected Homer was the robot
jokes.punchline: Bart's casual acceptance of Homer's chronic absence, Homer's cheerful agreement
jokes.explanation: Dark family dynamic - both characters casually accepting Homer's neglectful parenting
